name: Indian Steel Consumers (Auto & Construction Sector)
description: Represents the aggregate demand side — major steel-consuming industries in India including automotive manufacturers, construction companies, and infrastructure developers. These sectors account for ~75% of domestic steel consumption.
objectives: Secure stable and affordable steel supply for operations
Minimise raw material cost impact on product pricing
Maintain production schedules without supply disruptions
Pass through cost increases to end consumers only as a last resort
constraints: Price-sensitive end markets — cannot absorb unlimited cost increases
Long-term supply contracts limit short-term flexibility
Quality specifications — cannot easily switch to inferior alternatives
Inventory carrying costs — stockpiling is expensive
Dependent on domestic steel availability — import alternatives are costlier

automotive_context.ev_transition_impact: EV requires ~30% less steel per vehicle but more aluminium

switching_behaviour.can_switch_to_imports: Partially — quality certification and lead times are barriers
switching_behaviour.can_substitute_materials: Limited — aluminium/composites for some auto applications
switching_behaviour.can_defer_purchases: Up to 1-2 quarters before production impact
